# Integrating AI and IoT for Smarter Supply Chains

![Picture of Curtis Glesmann](https://stockiqtech.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/cropped-CG-StockIQ-600x600.jpg)

#### Curtis Glesmann

#### Table of Contents

TodayвЂ™s supply chains are far more than just networks that move goods from point A to point B. They are complex global systems that respond in realtime to data, insights, and change. In this ecosystem, there are two transformative technologies that, when properly integrated, have the power to revolutionize the way supply chains function: [artificial intelligence (AI)](https://stockiqtech.com/blog/ai-demand-planning/) and the Internet of Things (IoT).

AI and IoT in [supply chain management](https://stockiqtech.com/blog/improve-supply-chain-management-strategy/) are a powerhouse duo, providing companies with unprecedented real-time insights, predictive capabilities, intelligent analysis, and automated solutions. How exactly? IoT collects and transmits data from devices, machines, and sensors, while AI analyzes this data and makes intelligent decisions and suggestions.

HereвЂ™s how AI and IoT work together to transform supply chains.

## What are AI and IoT in Supply Chains?

To fully understand the role of AI and IoT in supply chains, letвЂ™s start by breaking down each technology.

### AI in Supply Chains

Artificial intelligence, or AI, is a branch of computer science that enables computers to learn, solve problems, and make decisions. In supply chains, AI is primarily used for tasks like data analysis, predictive modeling, and automating repetitive tasks. By processing vast amounts of both historical and real-time data, AI can anticipate expected demand, optimize inventory levels, and even predict potential disruptions.

AI is a powerful tool for enhancing supply chain performance, with studies showing that [top supply chain organizations](https://www.gartner.com/en/newsroom/press-releases/2024-02-20-gartner-says-top-supply-chain-organizations-are-using-ai-to-optimize-processes-at-more-than-twice-the-rate-of-low-performing-peers) are using AI to optimize processes at more than twice the rate of their low-performing peers.

### IoT in Supply Chains

The Internet of Things, or IoT, refers to a network of interconnected physical devices that collect and share data through sensors, software, and other technologies. In supply chains, IoT is vital for monitoring equipment, devices, and other вЂњthingsвЂќ in real time. For example, IoT devices on delivery trucks can monitor location, temperature, and humidity, providing critical information for transporting goods.

IoT adoption in supply chains is growing in popularity, and by 2033, the global market worth of IoT in supply chain management is [expected to exceed $40 billion](https://www.globaltrademag.com/iot-in-supply-chain-market-is-likely-to-witness-a-worth-of-us-41-8-billion-rising-at-a-cagr-of-12-9-from-2023-to-2033/).

## How AI and IoT Work Together in Supply Chain Management

While both AI and IoT in supply chains are essential technologies in their own right, the way they work together has the power to transform the way businesses operate. How exactly? IoT devices first provide a continuous stream of data from devices and sensors. Then, AI can collect and interpret this data to generate insights, make predictions, and even suggest decisions.

HereвЂ™s a closer look at how this integration drives innovation across supply chains:

### 1. Real-time visibility and monitoring

IoT devices, such as trackers and sensors, offer visibility into the exact condition, location, and status of machines and goods as they function and move through the supply chain in real time. AI then collects and leverages this data to generate actionable insights.

For example, a shipment of temperature-sensitive products equipped with IoT sensors can relay data to an AI-powered system that monitors temperature fluctuations. If an issue is detected, the system can trigger alerts, or even adjust the temperature to protect the goods.

### 2. Predictive maintenance and equipment health

AI and IoT in supply chains enable [predictive maintenance](https://stockiqtech.com/blog/predictive-maintenance/), which helps prevent equipment breakdowns and reduces downtime. Predictive maintenance is a proactive approach to equipment management and repair, which uses data and analytics to anticipate when equipment is likely to fail.

IoT sensors installed on machines collect data on metrics such as temperature, vibrations, and usage hours. Then, AI algorithms analyze these metrics to detect early signs of wear and predict when maintenance is needed. This means that instead of adhering to a fixed maintenance schedule (which might lead to unnecessary maintenance or breakdowns), companies can service equipment only when necessary.

### 3. Demand forecasting and inventory optimization

AIвЂ™s data analytics capabilities allow it to predict demand and make inventory recommendations based on a range of data inputs, including data collected from IoT sensors. For example, AI-based algorithms can predict anticipated demand for various products, while IoT-enabled sensors provide visibility into inventory levels at various warehouses. This allows companies to dynamically adjust ordering processes to align with real-time inventory levels with anticipated demand, reducing the risk of overstocking or stockouts.

### 4. Route optimization and fleet management

In logistics, IoT devices installed in vehicles continuously track factors like location, speed, fuel consumption, and road conditions. AI then processes this data to recommend optimized routes, reduce delivery times, and minimize fuel costs. For example, AI systems can suggest routes that bundle deliveries together, ensuring goods reach their destination as quickly and efficiently as possible.

The combination of AI and IoT enables companies to turn raw data into real-time, actionable insights that can streamline and strengthen supply chain operations.

## Tips for Successful Integration

Successfully integrating AI and IoT in supply chains requires thoughtful planning and integration. While you might already use some form of either IoT and/or AI within your business, adopting emerging forms of these technologies and using them together successfully likely involves addressing technological, operational, and strategic challenges.

Here are some practical tips to help ensure a successful integration of AI and IoT within your supply chain organization.

### 1. Determine your starting point

Your organization already has some sort of technological infrastructure in place, whether thatвЂ™s sensors on certain machinery, an [ERP system](https://stockiqtech.com/blog/erp/), or demand forecasting software. Whether youвЂ™re looking to overhaul your current systems, implement technologies for the first time, or launch some patch upgrades, itвЂ™s important to first understand your вЂњtechnological starting point.вЂќ What are your current systems like? Where are their gaps? How will new technology need to work in tandem with your previous capabilities?

### 2. Then, identify clear objectives

Before launching any new AI and IoT technologies, itвЂ™s crucial to outline clear objectives that align with your business goals, to monitor progress and achieve results. Are you aiming to reduce costs, improve delivery times, increase inventory accuracy, or enhance customer satisfaction? Setting specific goals will help you select the right technologies and evaluate the success of your integration.

Also, establish key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure progress, such as order accuracy, delivery lead time, and maintenance costs. Regularly monitoring and assessing these KPIs allows you to gauge the effectiveness of your AI-IoT integration and make adjustments as needed.

### 3. Invest in quality data collection and management systems

High-quality data is essential for both AI accuracy and IoT effectiveness. Because of this, itвЂ™s important to invest in systems and technologies that generate clean, accurate data, and which result in advanced insights. This might include advanced sensors and IoT systems, as well as supply chain management software which produces intelligent insights and [demand forecasts](https://stockiqtech.com/products/demand-planning/) from the raw data.

### 4. Train your teams

An effective AI-IoT integration requires collaboration between IT, operations, warehouse, and supply chain teams. These employees need to be well-trained on all necessary systems and technologies and need to work together to align on objectives, troubleshoot issues, and ensure systems meet operational needs. To maximize the effectiveness of these systems, involve end-users early in the integration process to understand their workflows, pain points, and training needs, to ensure the technology is both usable and impactful.

Integrating AI and IoT in supply chains can transform operations and unlock new efficiencies, but achieving success requires a well-planned approach. By understanding these technologies and prioritizing effective integration, companies can build a robust AI-IoT network that supports their supply chain needs.

### StockIQ: Your Partner for Seamless AI and IoT Integration

AI and IoT go hand-in-hand in todayвЂ™s supply chains. And if youвЂ™re ready to leverage the power of both of these technologies to take your business to the next level, weвЂ™re here to help. WeвЂ™re StockIQ, and our user-friendly supply chain management software allows you to control inventory, simplify ordering, and improve forecasting with ease.

# How AI and IoT is Used in Inventory Management

![AI in Supply Chain Tech Explainers logo](https://assets1.consumergoods.com/images/v/max_width_1440/2023-07/CGT_TECH_EXPLAINERS_Unpacked_Logo_1300__300_0723_v2.png)

Inventory management is a timeless challenge for consumer goods manufacturers and retailers, and the pandemic only made things more difficult. As a result, many companies are identifying it as one of the areas theyГўВЂВ™re most interested in supercharging through the use of technology.В 

In fact, lack of real-time inventory visibility was chosen by 53% of retailers as the top priority of ГўВЂВњhigh importanceГўВЂВќ that needs to be resolved through technology upgrades or implementations, according to [RIS NewsГўВЂВ™](https://risnews.com/supply-chain-tech-study-2022) Supply Chain Technology Study, making it a prime use case for [AI in the supply chain](/ai-supply-chain).В В В 

Both artificial intelligence and the Internet of Things have the ability to transform supply chains and logistics, though in different ways. What they do have in common, however, is that theyГўВЂВ™re still gaining in widespread use across the consumer goods industry.В 

ГўВЂВњIoT is still an emerging technology in CGs supply chains,ГўВЂВќ says Lindsey Peters, retail and consumer goods lead at Celonis. ГўВЂВњWhile it has optimized supply chain track/trace for decades now, the massive leaps forward in miniaturization and precision in this space have resulted in massive increases in the amount of data generated to monitor and track items moving through the supply chain.ГўВЂВќВ 

## **How is AI used in inventory management?**

In short, [AI and IoT in the supply chain](/ai-and-iot-supply-chain) can be used in inventory management to provide manufacturers and retailers with greater visibility into the location of their goods and the optimal way to manage and move them.В В 

When it comes to AI in supply chain use cases, C&A Modas is a prime example. The fashion retailer leveraged an AI-infused platform from Palantir to optimize and quicken its inventory process, from planning to shipping to retail stores, to improve inventory management of its best-selling products. The technology processed and generated purchase recommendations, accounting for variables influencing long-term inventory planning, such as sales seasonality, product performance variations, and financial criteria, among other things.В 

A digital twin of its logistics chain provided C&AГўВЂВ™s planning teams with a wider view of the purchasing process, as well as offered the ability to quickly simulate new rules and scenarios. As a result, the company increased sales of in-stock products and reduced unnecessary overstock, according to Bruno Ferreira, planning and business intelligence director for C&A Brazil

![What are the benefits of AI in inventory management?](https://assets1.consumergoods.com/images/v/max_width_1440/2023-07/ai_in_supply_chain_inventory_visibility.png)

## **What are the benefits of AI in inventory management?**

When looking at how the benefits of AI in supply chain trickle down to inventory management, the technology affords leaders visibility into a number of important factors, including:В 

Having this information means that companies can make decisions more quickly and accurately, as well as head off challenges more proactively.В 

ГўВЂВњThe use of full artificial intelligence would be valuable in identifying where to apply pattern recognition in the first place, potentially finding and fixing supply chain issues that are less obvious to humans, and identifying true root causes of issues where the human targeting method may be simply looking at another symptom in the chain,ГўВЂВќ notes Peters.В 

![AI in Supply Chain Newsletter Signup](https://assets1.consumergoods.com/images/v/max_width_1440/2023-07/ai_in_supply_chain_newsletter_sign_up_2.jpg)

## **How is IoT used in inventory management?**

CPG companies have traditionally used two varieties of IoT to track their inventory, says Steve Statler, chief marketing officer and ESG lead at Wiliot, a provider of IoT technology. The first includes RFID tag inventory sensors, which are stickers that can be read by handheld scanners to provide inventory information. The second is wireless inventory trackers, which leverage sensors to track containers using cellular, GPS, and Bluetooth.В 

A newer technology, known as ambient IoT, which Wiliot provides, combines physical tags (similar to RFID) with Bluetooth, Wi-Fi, and 5G, to provide more real-time data to manufacturers and retailers.В 

Adheer Bahulkar, global supply chain lead of AccentureГўВЂВ™s consumer goods and industry practice, notes that visibility into inventory along the extended value chain is a big challenge. This includes raw materials inventory from a supplier, the transit to the plants, finished goods inventory from plant warehouses to distribution centers, and all the way to customer warehouses and retail stores.В 

ГўВЂВњSince IoT can be deployed to real-time visibility on inventory at a case, pallet, and container level, it can be a very powerful mechanism to inform the business about the best decisions to make,ГўВЂВќ he says. ГўВЂВњReal-time visibility can help with inventory management, [such as] improve customer fill rates by being able to quickly scan what inventory is available where and making it available to promise.В 

IoT in supply chain can also reduce merchandise waste by ensuring the right inventory is being focused on for selling and deployment while the product still has a meaningful shelf-life, he adds.В 

## **How IoT can be used in warehouses**

Among the ways that IoT can be used in warehouses include monitoring temperature data in cold settings where food and/or other items must remain below a certain temperature level to stay compliant.В

FULL CONTENT:
The efficiency and agility of supply chain operations are more critical than ever. Companies are constantly seeking innovative solutions to streamline processes, reduce costs, and enhance customer satisfaction. One of the most promising advancements in this realm is [AI](/blog/what-are-the-four-types-of-ai). Pairing AI with the capabilities of IoT brings together a solution that is revolutionizing supply chain operations. Whether you are aiming to enhance your inventory management, improve logistics and delivery efficiency, or increase overall operational resilience, AI and IoT provide the tools to achieve these goals. Fleet management AI in particular has the potential to revolutionize supply chain management by offering unprecedented levels of insight, automation, and optimization. By harnessing the capabilities of IoT, companies can not only remain competitive but also create more resilient and adaptive supply chains capable of responding to the dynamic market landscape. LetвЂ™s delve into the myriad of ways AI and IoT can benefit your supply chain operations, highlighting the transformative power of this cutting-edge technology.В 

---

* **Loss Prevention.** Reduce the amount of loss that occurs during the supply chain process
* **Location Coverage.** AirFinder Everywhere uses a combination of GPS, Cellular, and WiFi to determine location everywhere
* **Security Alerts.** Know when a delay in shipment has occurred so the problem  
  can be addressed immediately.

## Automation

AI-powered automation is revolutionizing supply chains by significantly enhancing their efficiency, accuracy, and responsiveness. When AI collects and analyzes data collected from an IoT system like an [asset tracking](/asset-tracking) solution, it can use that data to make automated decisions. AI plays a pivotal role in enhancing decision-making processes by analyzing vast amounts of data to predict demand, optimize routes, and manage inventory. For example, AI algorithms can assess historical data collected by asset tracking and market trends to forecast future demand with high accuracy, ensuring that inventory levels are optimized and reducing the risk of stockouts or overstocking. This predictive capability allows companies to respond swiftly to changing market conditions and customer needs, ensuring a smoother and more responsive supply chain.

Asset tracking technology, enabled by the IoT, provides real-time visibility and monitoring of goods in transit. These tracking devices installed on trailers and their cargo can relay information about the location, speed, and condition of shipments. This real-time data is invaluable for fleet managers, allowing them to monitor the progress of deliveries, anticipate delays, and reroute shipments if necessary to avoid disruptions. Furthermore, advanced asset tracking systems can monitor environmental conditions such as temperature and humidity, ensuring that perishable goods are transported under optimal conditions. This level of monitoring helps in maintaining the quality and integrity of products, especially in industries like pharmaceuticals and food and beverage.

The synergy between AI and asset tracking technology significantly enhances supply chain efficiency and reliability. AI can process data from asset tracking systems to provide actionable insights, such as optimizing delivery routes based on real-time traffic conditions or identifying patterns that indicate potential issues before they escalate. This integration reduces operational costs, improves delivery times, and enhances customer satisfaction. Additionally, the automation of these processes minimizes the need for manual intervention, reducing human error and allowing personnel to focus on strategic initiatives.

## Data Insights

Data insights in the supply chain, powered by AI and IoT technology, are revolutionizing the way businesses manage and optimize their operations. AI algorithms can process and analyze massive volumes of data from various sources, including an [IoT platform](/iot-platform), to provide predictive analytics and actionable insights. For example, logistics AI can forecast demand more accurately by identifying patterns and trends that humans might miss, allowing companies to adjust their inventory levels proactively. This helps in minimizing both overstock and stockouts, ensuring that products are available when and where they are needed, thereby improving customer satisfaction and reducing carrying costs.

An IoT platform, such as asset tracking, offers real-time visibility into the location and status of assets throughout the supply chain. This technology allows companies to monitor the movement of goods from production through delivery, ensuring that each item is accounted for and arrives at its destination on time. By integrating asset tracking with AI, companies can gain deeper insights into their operations. For instance, AI can analyze data from asset tracking systems to optimize asset utilization. Often, trailers or other equipment go unknowingly unused for extended periods of time, leading to unnecessary wear and tear on the overused assets. Additionally, it can provide early warnings of potential disruptions, such as delays or equipment failures, allowing for quick corrective actions.

The combination of AI and asset tracking technology enhances supply chain transparency and efficiency. Real-time data from asset tracking systems can be fed into AI models to improve decision-making processes. For example, if an asset is delayed, AI can automatically reroute other assets to mitigate the impact on the overall supply chain. This reduces downtime and increases operational resilience. Furthermore, these technologies help in maintaining regulatory compliance by providing accurate and timely records of asset movements and conditions.

## Planning

Planning in the supply chain has become significantly more sophisticated and effective with the integration of IoT technology and AI in fleet management. AI in logistics excels at analyzing vast datasets, extracting patterns, and providing predictive insights that aid in strategic decision-making. For instance, AI can process historical data to forecast demand with high precision. This enables supply chain managers to plan inventory levels, production schedules, and deliveries more accurately, reducing the risks of stockouts or late deliveries. Accurate demand forecasting ensures that resources are allocated efficiently, ultimately improving service levels and reducing costs.

The visibility that asset tracking technology provides ensures that every asset is accounted for and helps in monitoring the condition of these raw materials, finished products, cargo, В and even trailers during transit. By integrating asset tracking data with AI, companies can enhance their planning processes even further. For example, [AI at the edge](/blog/artificial-intelligence-at-the-edge) can analyze real-time data from tracking systems to optimize logistics and transportation routes, ensuring timely deliveries and reducing transportation costs. The synergy between AI and asset tracking technology brings unprecedented levels of accuracy and responsiveness to supply chain planning. Real-time data from asset tracking systems, when processed by AI, provides actionable insights that enable dynamic planning. For example, if a shipment is delayed, AI can suggest alternative routes or reallocate resources to maintain the supply chain's flow. This flexibility helps in maintaining high service levels and minimizes downtime. Furthermore, these technologies facilitate better collaboration among stakeholders by providing a shared, accurate view of the supply chain status. This transparency enhances trust and coordination between suppliers, manufacturers, and distributors.

## Why This Technology is Important to Have In Supply Chains

Reduce Human Error

Reducing human error through the use of AI and IoT significantly benefits [logistics operations](/logistics-rtls-tracking) by enhancing accuracy, efficiency, and reliability. Human errors, often resulting from manual data entry, miscommunication, or oversight, can lead to costly mistakes such as incorrect shipments, inventory discrepancies, and delivery delays. AI and IoT work together to mitigate these issues by automating data analysis and decision-making processes. For instance, AI-powered systems can automatically capture and process data from IoT tracking devices, ensuring that information is consistently accurate and up-to-date. This reduces the likelihood of errors that can occur when data is manually entered or transferred between systems. By continuously learning from this data, AI can suggest the most efficient processes, minimizing delays. This not only improves delivery times but also reduces operational costs. Additionally, AI and IoT improve communication and coordination across the supply chain. Automated systems can send timely alerts and updates, ensuring that all stakeholders are informed and can respond quickly to any issues. This reduces the risk of miscommunication and ensures smoother, more coordinated logistics operations.

Increase Uptime

Increasing uptime through AI using data collected by an asset tracking system brings significant benefits to logistics operations. Uptime, the period when equipment is operational and functional, is crucial for logistics, as downtime negatively impacts efficiency and costs. AI solutions for logistics, when integrated with trailer tracking systems, harness real-time data to optimize fleet management. By continuously monitoring trailers, AI can predict and prevent potential failures, scheduling maintenance before issues escalate into costly breakdowns. This predictive maintenance ensures that trailers are almost always operational, reducing unplanned downtime and enhancing overall productivity. AI's role in data analysis allows for intelligent routing and scheduling. By analyzing traffic patterns, weather conditions, and historical data, AI can suggest optimal routes and delivery schedules, minimizing delays and fuel consumption. This efficiency not only cuts operational costs but also ensures timely deliveries, enhancing customer satisfaction and competitiveness in the market. Furthermore, AI for logistics provides insights into asset utilization, enabling managers to make informed decisions to improve operational efficiency, like rescheduling the cycle in which trailers are rotated. Moreover, AI can streamline administrative tasks associated with fleet management. Automated reporting and documentation reduce the workload on human staff, allowing them to focus on strategic initiatives rather than routine paperwork. The improved accuracy and timeliness of AI-generated reports also enhance decision-making and operational transparency.

Reduce Operational Costs

Decreasing costs through AI using data collected by an IoT tracking system brings substantial benefits to supply chain operations. Cost efficiency is paramount in supply chain management, where even minor savings can lead to significant financial gains. AI, integrated with asset tracking systems, enables continuous monitoring and analysis of trailers, optimizing their usage and maintenance schedules. By predicting maintenance needs and preventing unexpected breakdowns, AI minimizes downtime and repair costs, ensuring that trailers remain operational longer and reducing the frequency of expensive emergency repairs. AI's ability to analyze vast amounts of data in real time facilitates intelligent route planning and load optimization. Efficient routing not only conserves resources but also improves the reliability of deliveries, contributing to better customer satisfaction and retention. Additionally, AI-driven insights into driver behavior and fuel usage provide opportunities for further cost reductions. By monitoring and analyzing driving patterns, AI can identify inefficiencies such as excessive idling or harsh braking, offering recommendations for improvement and holding drivers accountable. Training drivers based on these insights can lead to more fuel-efficient driving practices, reducing fuel expenses and extending the lifespan of their trailers. AI also streamlines administrative processes by automating routine tasks such as scheduling, reporting, and compliance checks. Automation reduces the need for manual intervention, cutting labor costs and minimizing the risk of human error. Accurate and timely data generated by AI enhances decision-making, allowing supply chain managers to make informed, cost-effective choices.

## Getting The Most Out Of AI

AI needs data collected by an asset tracking system to be successful because this data provides the essential input for AI algorithms to function effectively. Asset tracking systems gather extensive real-time information on various parameters such as vehicle locations, asset conditions, routes, and even driver behavior. This continuous flow of data enables AI to perform complex analyses, identify patterns, and generate actionable insights that are crucial for optimizing fleet operations. The success of AI in fleet management hinges on the accuracy, volume, and timeliness of the data it processes. For instance, AI uses real-time location data to optimize routes, thereby reducing fuel consumption and improving delivery times. Without precise location data, AI cannot accurately predict the most efficient routes or adapt to changing traffic conditions, undermining its ability to enhance operational efficiency. Driver behavior data is another critical input for AI, helping to identify unsafe driving practices and areas for improvement. AI can analyze patterns such as harsh braking, rapid acceleration, and excessive idling to recommend training programs that enhance driver safety and fuel efficiency. This level of detailed analysis is only possible with the comprehensive data provided by asset tracking systems. Moreover, fleet tracking data supports AI in compliance and regulatory reporting by automatically generating accurate and timely reports, reducing the administrative burden and the risk of human error. The integration of asset tracking data with AI enables automated decision-making processes, allowing fleet managers to focus on strategic initiatives rather than routine operational tasks.

## Will You Use AI and IoT To Improve Your Supply Chain Operations?

AI stands as a transformative force in supply chain operations, offering unparalleled efficiencies and insights that drive business success. By leveraging data from IoT solutions like asset tracking, AI enhances decision-making through predictive analytics, ensuring that supply chains are not just reactive but proactive. AI enhances supply chain resilience by identifying potential disruptions and enabling businesses to develop robust contingency plans. In essence, AI paired with IoT empowers supply chain operations with enhanced visibility, efficiency, and agility, fostering a more responsive and sustainable business model.

As the technology continues to evolve, its integration into supply chain management will undoubtedly become more profound, cementing AI as an indispensable tool for driving operational excellence and competitive advantage in the modern business landscape. Embracing AI in supply chain operations is not just an option but a strategic imperative for forward-thinking businesses. The level of visibility it provides is unparalleled and essential to improving supply chain operations.
